confirm.title: Speaker Recognition App discovered
confirm.description: Connect Home Assistant to the running Speaker Recognition App? The discovered App is used as the recognition backend.

user.title: Set up Speaker Recognition
user.description: Configure a recognition backend, or add an STT/conversation proxy when the App is already connected.

data_description.url: Internal URL of the Speaker Recognition App or compatible backend.
data_description.token: Token used by Home Assistant to authenticate with the backend.

add_stt.title: Add STT proxy
add_stt.description: Wrap an existing speech-to-text entity so the transcript is preserved while the speaker is identified.

add_conversation.title: Add conversation proxy
add_conversation.description: Wrap an existing conversation agent and add one or more recognized speakers as untrusted personalization context. Voice recognition never changes Home Assistant users or permissions.

data_description.min_confidence: Below this confidence, no person context is added and the source agent is used normally.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the Speaker Recognition backend
error.recursive_proxy: A Speaker Recognition proxy cannot wrap another Speaker Recognition proxy
error.source_in_use: This source is already wrapped by another Speaker Recognition proxy

abort.already_configured: This Speaker Recognition App is already configured
abort.reconfigured: The existing Speaker Recognition integration now uses the confirmed App

init.title: Speaker Recognition options
init.description: Backend connection settings are validated before they are saved. Single- or multi-speaker context is personalization only and never authorization.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the Speaker Recognition backend
error.recursive_proxy: A Speaker Recognition proxy cannot wrap another Speaker Recognition proxy
error.source_in_use: This source is already wrapped by another Speaker Recognition proxy

abort.not_supported: This entry type is not supported
